component_identification.content: Base 2E includes 500 cards: 250 Kingdom cards (10 each of 25 types), 130 Treasure cards (60 Copper, 40 Silver, 30 Gold), 48 Victory cards (24 Estate, 12 Duchy, 12 Province), 30 Curse cards, 7 blank/randomizer cards, 1 Trash card, and rulebook/setup trays. Players start with 7 Copper + 3 Estate each.

core_game_loop.content: On your turn: Action phase, Buy phase, Clean-up phase. Play one Action by default (unless cards grant +Actions), then play any Treasures and buy one card with available Coins (unless +Buys). Discard played and unplayed cards, draw 5 new cards. Goal is to end with most victory points in deck.

detailed_rules.content: Setup uses 10 Kingdom piles plus base Treasure/Victory/Curse piles. Each card text is authoritative and can break baseline rules. Gaining puts cards in discard unless specified otherwise. Attack cards affect opponents unless they reveal/use a reaction that says it can defend. Trash is permanent removal and crucial for efficiency. Game ends immediately at end of current turn when Province pile is empty or any 3 supply piles are empty (4 piles in some larger-player variants/expansions). Victory points come from green cards and some Kingdom cards; deck quality matters because dead VP cards reduce draw quality during play. Important timing: resolve card instructions top-to-bottom, finish one card fully before next unless card says otherwise; duration-style effects are expansion-specific and not in base rules.

scoring_and_endgame.content: At game end, count all VP in each player’s full deck/discard/hand/in-play: Estate 1, Duchy 3, Province 6, Curse -1, plus any Kingdom VP cards in setup. Highest VP wins; tie goes to player with fewer turns, then shared if still tied.

beginner_strategy.content: 1) Buy enough economy first (Silvers, key draw cards) before greening. 2) Trashing weak starters (Estates/Coppers) is usually the biggest jump in deck power. 3) Don’t split between too many Kingdom plans; build around 1–2 strong synergies. 4) Track end triggers—sometimes emptying a third pile wins even without Province majority. 5) Start buying Provinces when your deck can keep buying them repeatedly.
